samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel data to serial format.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[2:0]">Counter register.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid" type_description="wire">Valid signal for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data" width_description="[3:0]">Data register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module parallel2serial converts parallel data to serial format. 
    It has 4-bit parallel data input (d) and serial data output (dout). 
    The output valid_out indicates the availability of serial output. 
    The module uses a counter (cnt) to control the conversion process. 
    When the counter reaches the value of 3, the module updates the data register with the parallel data input, resets the counter, and sets the valid signal to 1.
    Otherwise, the module increments the counter, sets the valid signal to 0, and shifts the data register left by 1 bit.
    </implementation>
</module>
